title: Question about seeing 30 in Japanese theaters
display_title: Question about seeing 30 in Japanese theaters
wikitext:
[]: Question about seeing 3.0 in Japanese theaters             Quote Post by spiderman is cool » Sat Nov 10, 2012 1:12 am Hello, Longtime lurker. I've been living in Japan (Okinawa Prefecture) for almost a year now, and have yet to actually go to a Japanese movie theater. There is on on Kokusai street in Naha that will be playing 3.0 next week, but my question is for anyone that has lived in Japan is should I buy tickets online prior to the showing? As far as I understand this is the only theater in Okinawa that will be showing it. I do not know how many showings movies typically get in Japanese theaters, is it exactly like seeing a movie in the States? Also my Japanese is minimal... I know barely enough to get by, I understand that I won't exactly understand the whole movie but I am NOT going to pass up the chance to see an Evangelion movie in theaters in Japan in my lifetime. Anyways, any advice anyone has would be helpful!

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Nov 10, 2012 3:11 am First of all, welcome to EGF!!! I hope you enjoy your time here! As for your question: With a movie this big, I would definitely look into buying a ticket online. There's usually a small service fee for online ticketing, but it's worth it to not have that extra stress of looking for a ticket on the 17th. You're lucky among Eva fans, my friend. I hope all goes well in your quest to see 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Nov 10, 2012 3:19 am I would reserve the ticket now, the online service is your best shot to beat out other Japanese Eva fans. Something tells me that finding a ticket on the 17th would be next to impossible. Also welcome to EGF!

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat Nov 10, 2012 7:11 am Regarding reserving tickets online; just check the site of the cinema in question. The one I'm hitting up only has pre-sales for the dates of the 17th and 18th respectively - Whereas I'm not seeing it until the 22nd. Also, for what it's worth: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13055/Seeing-Evangelion-Q-30-in-Japan/0/?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 10, 2012 10:03 am I would reserve tickets beforehand as its possible they'll be sold out if you only try to walk in screening. As for Japanese theatre etiquette: shut the fuck up don't munch shit in loud noise either stay up till end of credits (well to be fair with Rebuild that's pretty much required, given post-credit scenes) but basically Japanese audiences are very conscious about not making noise and disrupting the viewing experience of others. They also tend to watch credits till end.

[]: Re: Question about seeing 3.0 in Japanese theaters             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Nov 10, 2012 10:28 am spiderman is cool wrote: I've been living in Japan (Okinawa Prefecture) for almost a year now, and have yet to actually go to a Japanese movie theater. There is on on Kokusai street in Naha that will be playing 3.0 next week

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Nov 10, 2012 5:33 pm Xard wrote: As for Japanese theatre etiquette: shut the fuck up don't munch shit in loud noise either stay up till end of credits (well to be fair with Rebuild that's pretty much required, given post-credit scenes) but basically Japanese audiences are very conscious about not making noise and disrupting the viewing experience of others. They also tend to watch credits till end.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 10, 2012 5:43 pm A.T. Fish wrote: I envy this.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 10, 2012 5:46 pm Xard wrote: Plus they got the best anti camrip/piracy ad ever

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Nov 10, 2012 5:50 pm Xard wrote: Yeah, I really enjoyed the experience myself last year. Plus they got the best anti camrip/piracy ad ever

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 10, 2012 5:57 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Which makes me wonder: did they record it inside a theatre?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Nov 10, 2012 7:11 pm ^Uncanny Valley INC. With stuff like this, I'm not expecting a camrip anytime soon.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 10, 2012 7:17 pm ^ Tines recently mentioned that the first rip came around the first overseas releases.

[]: Re: Question about seeing 3.0 in Japanese theaters             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Sat Nov 10, 2012 11:22 pm spiderman is cool wrote: I do not know how many showings movies typically get in Japanese theaters, is it exactly like seeing a movie in the States?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Nov 11, 2012 12:09 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Tines recently mentioned that the first rip came around the first overseas releases.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Nov 11, 2012 12:54 am Never mind my previous post, if anyone happened to read it, anyway by searching around I see that the camrip got released in July while the film was screened on June, it didn't take that much time, maybe it was some early screening if it came from overseas like Tines said. It still doesn't necessarily say anything about the next film but hopefully it won't really take six months.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Nov 11, 2012 1:01 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Never mind my previous post, if anyone happened to read it, anyway by searching around I see that the camrip got released in July while the film was screened on June, it didn't take that much time, maybe it was some early screening if it came from overseas like Tines said. It still doesn't necessarily say anything about the next film but hopefully it won't really take six months.

[]: Re: Question about seeing 3.0 in Japanese theaters             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Nov 11, 2012 2:18 am spiderman is cool wrote: There is on on Kokusai street in Naha that will be playing 3.0 next week, but my question is for anyone that has lived in Japan is should I buy tickets online prior to the showing? As far as I understand this is the only theater in Okinawa that will be showing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Amitch1991 » Sun Nov 11, 2012 6:42 am For a movie that is going to be as big as Eva 3.0, i would defenetly recommend booking tickets online or something :) (If it is possible to do this of course, i really don't know how Japanese cinemas work to be honest)

[]: First time big time!             Quote Post by Silentgold » Sun Nov 11, 2012 7:24 am Good day everyone. I'm going on a trip to Japan on November 21-30. Which means I get to watch the showing of 3.0. I'm just wondering, do they show subtitles in the theaters? Thank you. Anyway subtitle or not, I will still watch it. Haha
